description: Update the backend redesign references that currently conflict on resource-key seeding so this story has one explicit contract: `dms.ResourceKey` and `EffectiveSchemaInfo.ResourceKeysInIdOrder` include abstract resources and concrete non-extension resources, but exclude `isResourceExtension=true` overlay schemas because those compile into `_ext` tables on the owning base resource. Acceptance: `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/00-effective-schema-hash/03-resourcekey-seed.md` no longer says every concrete `resourceSchema` is seeded without the overlay exception, and any directly conflicting manifest/testing guidance is updated to match the relational-model design.
steps-to-verify: Review the updated wording in `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/00-effective-schema-hash/03-resourcekey-seed.md` and any touched companion design docs to confirm the exclusion of `isResourceExtension=true` overlays is explicit.
Run: `rg -n ¥

reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/00-effective-schema-hash/03-resourcekey-seed.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/ddl-generator-testing.md reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/08-derived-relational-model-set-builder.md`

description: Update relational-model startup validation so the completeness universe matches the production seed contract instead of every `projectSchema.resourceSchemas` entry. Acceptance: `RelationalModelSetValidation` excludes only `isResourceExtension=true` resources from the missing-resource-key check, still includes non-extension resources from extension projects and all abstract resources, and preserves the existing duplicate-id, duplicate-resource, extra-resource, and abstractness diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage for an effective schema set that contains a real extension-project resource and an `isResourceExtension=true` overlay, then assert validation requires the real resource key but does not require the overlay key.
Add a negative unit test proving a missing non-extension resource key or an extra unknown resource key still fails with the expected deterministic message.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ¥

description: Align fixture-generated `EffectiveSchemaInfo` and authoritative expected manifests with the same resource-key contract used at runtime. Acceptance: `EffectiveSchemaSetFixtureBuilder` stops synthesizing keys for `isResourceExtension=true` overlays, authoritative `effective-schema` outputs regenerate with matching `resource_key_count`, `resource_key_seed_hash`, and `resource_keys[]`, overlay entries such as `Sample:Contact`, `Sample:School`, `Sample:Staff`, `Sample:Student`, and similar TPDM overlays disappear, and true extension-project resources remain present.
steps-to-verify: Add or update fixture-builder tests proving synthetic `ResourceKeysInIdOrder` excludes overlay resources while keeping non-extension extension-project resources.
Regenerate the affected authoritative manifests and inspect the diff to confirm only overlay resource-key entries, counts, ids, and derived hashes changed.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend PostgreSQL startup/runtime mapping tests so this bug is covered by an extension-bearing schema instead of only a core-only fixture. Acceptance: `PostgresqlRuntimeMappingInitializationTests` or equivalent covers one success path where extension overlays are present and startup mapping initialization completes, plus one negative path where a real resource-key mismatch still fails fast with the underlying compile-time exception rather than a generic readiness symptom.
steps-to-verify: Add a startup or runtime-compiler test that loads a core schema plus extension schemas containing `isResourceExtension=true` overlays and asserts backend mapping initialization succeeds.
Add a negative startup or compiler-path test proving a missing key for a true non-extension resource still fails with an actionable error message.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName‾PostgresqlRuntimeMappingInitialization`

description: Wire the backend mapping phase into the existing startup-status signal so pre-bind compile failures record the real failing phase and summarized error before process exit. Acceptance: the status file transitions through `Starting` and `Completed` for `InitializeBackendMappings` on success, records `Failed` with the backend mapping exception summary on compile failure, and preserves existing fatal-startup behavior and ready-state reporting.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit coverage around the startup orchestrator and startup-status signal proving a backend mapping failure writes `Failed` for `InitializeBackendMappings` before the host exits.
Add or update a success-path test proving the status signal still reaches `Ready` after backend mapping initialization completes.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ¥

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName‾StartupStatus`

description: Update `.github/workflows/on-dms-pullrequest.yml` so readiness or OpenAPI-download failures surface the startup-status file and DMS container logs inline instead of only timing out on `localhost:8080`. Acceptance: when `Wait for DMS to be ready` fails, the workflow prints `/tmp/dms-startup-status.json` if present, emits the `dms-local-dms` log inline, uploads the relevant container logs as an artifact, and leaves successful runs unchanged through `if: failure()` guards.
steps-to-verify: Force a failing startup after `Start DMS Docker` and confirm the workflow log shows the startup phase and summarized backend error before the readiness timeout context.
Confirm the failing job uploads the DMS and companion container logs as artifacts and that successful runs skip the failure-only diagnostics steps.
Validate the edited workflow YAML in CI or with the repo's normal workflow review process before merge.

description: Align fatal startup status signaling with the orchestrator's cancellation contract so `OperationCanceledException` is not recorded as a fatal startup failure. Acceptance: `StartupPhaseExecutor.RunFatalAsync` must rethrow `OperationCanceledException` without writing `Failed`, without requesting process exit, and without overwriting the most recent non-failure startup phase status; real exceptions must continue to write `Failed` with the configured phase and summary before exit. Add unit coverage proving backend-mapping cancellation preserves non-fatal behavior while ordinary failures still emit `Failed` and exit.
steps-to-verify: Add a frontend startup-status unit test that invokes `StartupPhaseExecutor.RunFatalAsync` with an action that throws `OperationCanceledException`, then assert no exit is requested and the status file is not transitioned to `Failed` for `InitializeBackendMappings`.
Keep or extend the existing failure-path test to assert a non-cancellation exception still writes `Failed` for `InitializeBackendMappings` and requests process exit.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName‾StartupStatus`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName‾DmsStartupOrchestrator`

description: Remove the duplicated effective-schema normalization/hash/resource-key derivation path between `LoadAndBuildEffectiveSchemaTask` and PostgreSQL backend mapping compilation so startup and backend mapping consume one authoritative `EffectiveSchemaSet` contract. Acceptance: backend mapping initialization must reuse the effective schema data already produced during startup instead of rebuilding it from raw API schema nodes; the runtime mapping-set key and compiled mapping set must be derived from that shared authoritative startup state; schema normalization, effective-schema hash derivation, and resource-key seed derivation logic must not exist in two independent startup code paths with separately formatted failure handling. Preserve the current extension-aware resource-key behavior and actionable compile-time diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Refactor the backend mapping startup path so `PostgresqlRuntimeMappingSetCompiler` (or its replacement) receives the already-initialized effective schema contract rather than recomputing it from `IApiSchemaProvider`.

Add or update unit coverage proving backend mapping initialization uses the same effective-schema metadata established during the API-schema startup phase and still fails fast with actionable errors when the authoritative startup schema is invalid or missing.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ¥
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ¥

description: Harden grouped same-site reference projection compilation so duplicate `ReferenceIdentityBinding.ReferenceJsonPath` members must converge on both the same logical storage value and the same per-site presence semantics instead of only the same canonical storage column. Acceptance: `ReferenceIdentityProjectionLogicalFieldResolver` or equivalent plan-validation code must reject any grouped logical reference field whose member columns resolve to different `ColumnStorage.UnifiedAlias.PresenceColumn` values, or whose alias presence gate does not match the owning `DocumentReferenceBinding.FkColumn` when the design requires the reference FK to be the presence column. The failure must be deterministic and must identify the reference object path, grouped logical field path, table, and the conflicting member columns/presence columns. Existing valid grouped-reference and key-unified projection scenarios must continue to compile unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add a focused unit test in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ReadPlanCompilerTests.cs` that builds one grouped same-site reference binding where duplicate logical-field members share the same canonical storage column but use different presence gates, and assert read-plan compilation fails with an actionable deterministic message.

Add a second negative test proving a grouped same-site reference binding is rejected when one member's unified-alias presence gate does not match the owning `DocumentReferenceBinding.FkColumn` for that reference site.
Keep or extend the existing positive grouped-reference tests so a valid same-site grouped reference field with matching storage and presence gating still compiles to one logical projected field.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ¥

description: Preserve phase-specific in-progress startup summaries in the startup-status file instead of overwriting them with the generic text `Starting {phase}.`. Acceptance: the startup status written for `Starting` state during `StartupPhaseExecutor.RunFatalAsync` must use caller-supplied phase-specific wording, and the bootstrap startup helpers in `Program.cs` must do the same so early startup phases and hosted startup phases expose equally specific in-progress diagnostics. Add unit coverage proving a running backend-mapping phase records its configured descriptive summary while the phase is still in progress, and preserve the existing `Completed`, `Failed`, cancellation, and exit behavior.
steps-to-verify: Update the startup-status execution path so the `Starting` record for hosted phases such as `InitializeBackendMappings` can carry a specific summary rather than the hardcoded `Starting {phase}.` text.

Update the bootstrap startup helpers in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Program.cs` so the pre-build phases also write their caller-supplied start summaries consistently.
Add or update frontend startup-status unit tests to assert that while a `RunFatalAsync` phase is blocked/in progress, the status file contains `State = ¥
`, the expected phase name, and the configured descriptive summary for that phase.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName‾StartupStatus`
